posts.author: Dr Sharath Kumar G
posts.text: New in The Lancet: The ATLAS IPD meta-analysis (N=1,886) confirms endovascular thrombectomy improves 90-day functional outcomes (OR 1.63) and reduces mortality (31.1% vs 37.3%) in large-core ischemic stroke up to 24 hours from onset! Safety: Clinical benefit outweighs

posts.author: NeurologyLive®
posts.text: Experts are highlighting how advanced neuroimaging is reshaping stroke intervention decisions. Vascular neurologist Firas Bounni, MD, discusses thrombectomy and the future of personalized stroke care. #Stroke #Neurology #BrainHealth

posts.author: Mission Thrombectomy
posts.text: Should neuroprotection still matter in the thrombectomy era? The discussion continues. Interesting presentation highlighting the potential role of adjunctive neuroprotective strategies alongside mechanical thrombectomy.

posts.author: Mission Thrombectomy
posts.text: Remedy Robotics & Neuroendovascular Innovation: Expanding Access Beyond Borders. Dr. David Bell — From early first-in-human experience (~10 cases, including remote interventions) to the evolving vision of robotic-assisted stroke care.

posts.author: NEJM
posts.text: For patients with acute ischemic stroke, endovascular thrombectomy has improved outcomes in large-vessel occlusions, but its benefit in medium-vessel occlusions remains contentious. Research from the ORIENTAL-MeVO trial summarized in a new Quick Take video.

queries.name: cerebral AVM OR intracranial aneurysm OR endovascular

posts.author: Dr Sharath Kumar G
posts.text: Superior cerebellar artery (SCA) aneurysms are exceptionally rare (~0.2% incidence), and fusiform variants are even rarer. For ruptured dissecting distal SCA aneurysms, endovascular parent artery occlusion offers a highly effective treatment with low morbidity.
